title: ECB research agenda
summary: This page is an institutional overview of the ECB's research agenda structure, describing research priority areas rather than presenting specific research findings, data, or policy conclusions. It lists seven broad research themes including monetary policy, fiscal policy, international macro, labour markets, forecasting, macro-finance, and financial institutions. No specific research outputs, data releases, or policy recommendations are contained in this source.
key_points: Research is coordinated by the Research Coordination Committee with annual reporting to the Executive Board

Seven research strands are identified: monetary policy strategy/implementation, fiscal policy within EMU, international macro and finance, real sector/microeconomic analysis, forecasting and business cycle analysis, macro-finance and macroprudential policy, and financial institutions/microprudential policy
Research is conducted by Directorate General Research together with other ECB business areas
Research groups are organized by topic with members from across the organization
No specific research findings, data, or policy conclusions are presented on this page
why_it_matters: This page provides no actionable intelligence. It is an organizational descriptor of the ECB's research structure rather than a source of market-moving information or research outputs. It has no relevance for trading, risk management, or policy anticipation.
